title: Module 1 Project: Inventory Correction and Cleanup
summary: Correct audited inventory values and remove only two approved test records through six cumulative steps.

sketchBlocks.title: Inventory Correction and Cleanup Overview
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
In this project, you will take on the role of an Inventory Data Steward tasked with correcting and cleaning up an inventory database. An audit has identified incorrect prices, unfinished statuses, and internal test rows that need to be addressed. Your goal is to create a SQL script that precisely targets these issues without affecting legitimate records.

The project will guide you through a series of steps where you'll preview the data, apply updates, and verify changes. This ensures that only the intended records are modified or removed, preserving the integrity of the inventory data.

### Example Scenario
Consider an inventory item with an incorrect price and status. You'll write a SQL query to update these fields based on specific conditions, ensuring that only the targeted item is affected. This approach prevents accidental changes to other records, maintaining data accuracy.

By the end of this project, you'll have a comprehensive script that not only corrects errors but also removes test data, leaving the inventory clean and accurate.

quizDraft.title: Preview the Audit Targets
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SELECT statement to preview the inventory items with ids 2, 4, and 6. Include columns for price, status, is_test, and notes to review these items before any changes.
quizDraft.hint: Use a WHERE clause to filter the specific ids.

help.concept: Previewing data before making changes helps ensure that only the intended records are targeted.
help.hint_1: Use SELECT with a WHERE clause to filter specific ids.
help.hint_2: Ensure you include all necessary columns in your SELECT statement.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Write your SELECT statement here
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (2, 4, 6);
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT id, price, status, is_test, notes FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (2, 4, 6);

starterFiles.content:
-- Write your SELECT statement here
SELECT * FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (2, 4, 6);

solutionFiles.content: SELECT id, price, status, is_test, notes FROM inventory_items WHERE id IN (2, 4, 6);

quizDraft.title: Correct One Item Precisely
quizDraft.prompt: Update the inventory item with id 2 to set the price to 8.25 and status to 'active'. Ensure your query only affects this specific item.
quizDraft.hint: Use a WHERE clause to target the specific id.

help.concept: Using a WHERE clause ensures that only the intended record is updated.
help.hint_1: Include both SET and WHERE clauses in your UPDATE statement.
help.hint_2: Ensure the WHERE clause targets only id 2.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Preview the target
SELECT id, price, status FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 2;

-- Update the item
-- TODO: Write your UPDATE statement here

starterFiles.content:
-- Preview the target
SELECT id, price, status FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 2;

-- Update the item
-- TODO: Write your UPDATE statement here

quizDraft.title: Apply a Controlled Category Update
quizDraft.prompt: Update all non-test Decor items to set last_updated to '2026-02-10'. Ensure your query only affects the intended items.
quizDraft.hint: Use a WHERE clause to filter by category and is_test flag.

help.concept: Controlled updates ensure only the intended records are modified.
help.hint_1: Use WHERE to filter by category and is_test flag.
help.hint_2: Ensure your WHERE clause excludes test items.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Preview the target
SELECT id, category, is_test, last_updated FROM inventory_items WHERE category = 'Decor' AND is_test = 0;

-- Update the items
-- TODO: Write your UPDATE statement here

starterFiles.content:
-- Preview the target
SELECT id, category, is_test, last_updated FROM inventory_items WHERE category = 'Decor' AND is_test = 0;

-- Update the items
-- TODO: Write your UPDATE statement here

quizDraft.title: Delete One Confirmed Test Item
quizDraft.prompt: Delete the inventory item with id 4. Ensure your query only affects this specific item.
quizDraft.hint: Use a WHERE clause to target the specific id.

help.concept: Using a WHERE clause ensures that only the intended record is deleted.
help.hint_1: Include a WHERE clause in your DELETE statement.
help.hint_2: Ensure the WHERE clause targets only id 4.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Preview the target
SELECT id, name FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 4;

-- Delete the item
-- TODO: Write your DELETE statement here

starterFiles.content:
-- Preview the target
SELECT id, name FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 4;

-- Delete the item
-- TODO: Write your DELETE statement here

quizDraft.title: Remove the Remaining Draft Test Row
quizDraft.prompt: Delete the inventory item with id 6 only if it is marked as a test and has a draft status. Ensure your query only affects this specific item under these conditions.
quizDraft.hint: Use a WHERE clause with multiple conditions.

help.concept: Combining conditions in a WHERE clause ensures precise targeting of records.
help.hint_1: Use AND to combine conditions in your WHERE clause.
help.hint_2: Ensure both is_test and status conditions are met.

quizDraft.starterCode:
-- Preview the target
SELECT id, name, is_test, status FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 6;

-- Delete the item
-- TODO: Write your DELETE statement here

starterFiles.content:
-- Preview the target
SELECT id, name, is_test, status FROM inventory_items WHERE id = 6;

-- Delete the item
-- TODO: Write your DELETE statement here

quizDraft.title: Complete the Operational Handoff
quizDraft.prompt: Create a cumulative script that retains legitimate rows, contains the corrected values, and removes only the two approved test records. Ensure all previous steps are included.
quizDraft.hint: Ensure all previous updates and deletions are included in the script.

help.concept: A cumulative script ensures all intended changes are applied in sequence.
help.hint_1: Include all previous steps in your script.
help.hint_2: Ensure the final state matches the intended corrections and deletions.
